title: Contact lens-related complaint
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped vision, severe-pain, photophobia, corneal, redness, and contact-lens warning fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

red_flags.text: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped urgent return and ophthalmology context without adding treatment.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Identifies microbial keratitis as sight-threatening and supports urgent ophthalmology documentation; treatment specifics are not mapped.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Microbial keratitis (bacterial, fungal)

exact_section.heading: Patient summary — sight-threatening emergency and referral

referral.text: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped contact-lens cessation, hygiene, response review, and follow-up documentation.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped contact-lens history, red eye, discharge, vision, corneal concern, lens fit or hygiene, and visual-acuity documentation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-documented temporary lens cessation, hand and lens-case hygiene, avoidance of overnight wear, wearing-schedule review, and reassessment context.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped lens hygiene, case care, overnight-wear, wearing-schedule, cessation, and reassessment documentation.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented temporary lens cessation, hand and lens-case hygiene, avoidance of overnight wear, wearing-schedule review, and reassessment context.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Keratitis, contact-lens-associated infiltrative

exact_section.heading: Patient summary — lens cessation, hygiene, and wearing schedules

safety_netting.text: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports mapped contact-lens, unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, and corneal-opacity fields.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports contact-lens use, usually unilateral redness, watering, foreign-body sensation, discomfort, photophobia, peripheral corneal opacity, and the need to distinguish sight-threatening microbial keratitis.

source.title: Clinical Management Guideline: Keratitis, contact-lens-associated infiltrative

exact_section.heading: Clinical summary — presentation and differential safety

safety_netting.text: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports mapped severe pain, blurred vision, discharge, and sight-threatening infection warning context.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Supports usually unilateral acute redness, moderate or severe pain, photophobia, watering, discharge, blurred vision, corneal opacity or ulcer, and contact-lens, injury, surgery, exposure, dry-eye, or immune-risk context.

source_limitations: CLARE, broad contact-lens intolerance, dry-eye contacts, trauma and chemical negatives, eyelid and pupil assessment, fluorescein, red reflex, fundus, fields, medication selection, and fixed review timing remain unsupported.
Keratitis or infection must not be inferred automatically.

curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
